title: Charged Particle Track Reconstruction
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Charged Particle Track Reconstruction: Charged particle tracking: reconstruct helical track parameters from silicon detector hits using Kalman filter. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.kalman.track_filter…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~200; alignment_uncertainty_um, B_field_map_error_T set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: x_pred_k = F_k * x_{k-1} + noise; H_k * x_k = m_k (measurement); helix in B-field
E.world_state_x: track state vector (d0, phi0, pt, dz0, tan_lambda) at perigee
E.observation_y: silicon strip/pixel hit positions {u_k, v_k} at N_layers layers

W.regime: Existence of the recovered track_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 200); alignment_uncertainty_um dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: sigma(pt)/pt < 0.01% * pt/GeV for ATLAS-like detector. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~200. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |alignment_uncertainty_um|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_layers * N_tracks * N_iter) per event per iteration
